content:
How to Apply to University of South Africa (UNISA)

Thinking about studying at University of South Africa (UNISA)? Based in Pretoria (Distance Learning), Gauteng (National reach), UNISA is a Comprehensive University (Distance Learning) established in 1873. This guide covers everything you need to know to apply successfully — from APS requirements to application deadlines, bursaries, and step-by-step instructions.

At a Glance: UNISA Fast Facts

DetailInformation
Full NameUniversity of South Africa (UNISA)
TypeComprehensive University (Distance Learning)
LocationDistance learning — study from anywhere in South Africa
Year Founded1873
Application Portalhttps://www.unisa.ac.za/sites/myunisa/default/Apply-to-UNISA
Application FeeR115 per module (registration fee)
Applications OpenAugust (for following year)
Applications CloseNovember 30 (Semester 1); May (Semester 2)
Minimum APS23 (varies by programme)
Contact Phone012 429 3111
Contact Emailstudy-info@unisa.ac.za

Why Choose UNISA?

Largest open distance learning university in Africa; top 10 in the world by enrolment.

  • Largest university in Africa with over 400,000 students enrolled annually
  • Africa's oldest university, founded in 1873 as the University of the Cape of Good Hope
  • Full-time distance learning means you study from home — no campus attendance required
  • UNISA produces more graduates than any other South African university

Faculties and Programmes at UNISA

UNISA offers qualifications across 10 faculties:

  • Accounting Sciences
  • Agriculture & Environmental Sciences
  • Economic & Management Sciences
  • Education
  • Engineering & Technology
  • Graduate School of Business Leadership
  • Humanities
  • Law
  • Science, Engineering & Technology
  • Theology & Religion

Popular programmes include: BCom Accounting, LLB, BEd, BSc, BA Psychology.

APS Requirements for UNISA

The minimum Admission Point Score (APS) required at UNISA is 23 points. However, individual programmes may require significantly higher scores:

APS range: Most programmes open enrolment; some professional degrees require 28+

UNISA's minimum APS is 23 for most programmes. As a distance university, most programmes use open enrolment. Professional degrees (Law, Accounting) have stricter requirements. UNISA is ideal for working adults and students who cannot relocate.

How to Calculate Your APS

Your APS is calculated from your Grade 12 (NSC) subject results. Each subject receives a score from 1 to 7 based on your percentage:

Percentage (%)Rating CodeAPS Points
80–100%7 (Outstanding)7
70–79%6 (Meritorious)6
60–69%5 (Substantial)5
50–59%4 (Adequate)4
40–49%3 (Moderate)3
30–39%2 (Elementary)2
0–29%1 (Not Achieved)1

Your APS is the sum of your six best subjects (excluding Life Orientation, which is counted at half-value at most universities, or excluded entirely).

Use our free APS Calculator to calculate your score instantly.

Step-by-Step: How to Apply to UNISA

Step 1: Check Programme Requirements

Before applying, confirm the specific APS requirements and subject prerequisites for your chosen programme at https://www.unisa.ac.za. Some programmes require specific subjects (e.g., Mathematics for Engineering, Life Sciences for Medicine).

Step 2: Gather Your Documents

You will typically need:

  • Certified copy of your ID (South African ID or passport for international students)
  • Certified copy of your Grade 11 final results (if applying before matric)
  • Certified copy of your NSC / Grade 12 results (final matric results when available)
  • Proof of residence (e.g., utility bill)
  • Completed application form (online or paper)

Step 3: Apply Online

Visit the UNISA online application portal at https://www.unisa.ac.za/sites/myunisa/default/Apply-to-UNISA. Create an account, complete the application form, upload your documents, and pay the application fee of R115 per module (registration fee).

Step 4: Submit Before the Deadline

Applications for 2027 admission typically open in August (for following year) with intake in November 30 (Semester 1); May (Semester 2). Apply as early as possible — popular programmes fill up quickly, especially Medicine, Law, and Engineering.

Step 5: Accept Your Offer

Once you receive a conditional or unconditional offer from UNISA, accept it within the specified timeframe (usually 2–4 weeks). You may need to pay a registration deposit to secure your place.

Step 6: Register for Your Programme

After accepting your offer, complete the formal registration process in January (or mid-year for Semester 2 programmes). Bring original and certified copies of all documents.

Application Deadlines for UNISA

Missing the application deadline is one of the most common reasons students don't get into their chosen institution. Here are the key dates:

  • Applications Open: August (for following year)
  • Application Deadline: November 30 (Semester 1); May (Semester 2)
  • Results Notification: Typically November–December for the following year
  • Registration: January (Semester 1) or mid-year

Pro tip: Don't wait until the last day. Apply when applications open — some programmes are filled on a first-come, first-served basis.

Bursaries and Financial Aid at UNISA

Studying at UNISA can be financed through several bursaries and financial aid options:

  • NSFAS (for qualifying students)
  • UNISA Bursary Scheme
  • Employer-sponsored study leave
  • SASSETA bursaries

NSFAS (National Student Financial Aid Scheme): If your combined household income is below R350,000 per year, you may qualify for NSFAS funding. Apply at www.nsfas.org.za from October each year for the following academic year.

Preparing for UNISA While Still in School

The best time to prepare for university is while you're still in Grade 10, 11, or 12. Here's what you can do:

  • Focus on your key subjects: Make sure you're strong in the subjects required for your chosen programme
  • Use AI tutoring: StudyBuddy's AI tutor helps you master difficult CAPS topics before matric
  • Research your options early: Visit open days at UNISA to understand campus life
  • Apply for bursaries early: Some bursaries (like Allan Gray, Anglo American) are available while you're still in Grade 11
  • Track your APS: Use our APS calculator to know where you stand

What Happens After You Apply?

After submitting your application to UNISA, the process typically follows these stages:

  1. Application Confirmation: You'll receive an email or SMS confirming receipt of your application
  2. Conditional Offer: If your Grade 11 results meet the requirements, you may receive a conditional offer
  3. Final Results Submission: Submit your final NSC results in January
  4. Unconditional Offer: If your matric results meet or exceed the requirements, your offer becomes unconditional
  5. Registration: Register for your programme and pay your fees or confirm NSFAS/bursary funding

Comparing UNISA With Other Universities

Not sure if UNISA is the right fit? Here are some things to consider:

FactorUNISATypical Alternative
TypeComprehensive University (Distance Learning)Traditional University / UoT
LocationPretoria (Distance Learning)Varies
Minimum APS2320–37 (varies widely)
Application FeeR115 per module (registration fee)R100–R400
NSFAS EligibleYesYes (public) / No (private)

Frequently Asked Questions About Applying to UNISA

Still have questions? Our FAQ section below answers the most common questions about applying to UNISA.

Need extra help with your Grade 12 subjects to meet UNISA's entry requirements? StudyBuddy's AI tutor is FREE to start — get personalised help in Mathematics, Sciences, English, and all CAPS subjects. Most students improve their marks by 20–30% within a few weeks.
keywords: University of South Africa (UNISA) how to apply
quickAnswer: To apply to University of South Africa (UNISA), visit https://www.unisa.ac.za/sites/myunisa/default/Apply-to-UNISA, pay the R115 per module (registration fee) application fee, and submit your documents before November 30 (Semester 1); May (Semester 2). Minimum APS is 23 for most programmes.

faqs.question: What is the minimum APS to apply to UNISA?
faqs.answer: The minimum APS at UNISA is 23 points for most programmes. However, competitive programmes like Medicine, Law, or Engineering may require significantly higher scores (Most programmes open enrolment; some professional degrees require 28+). Always check the specific requirements for your chosen programme on the UNISA website.

faqs.question: When do applications open for UNISA?
faqs.answer: Applications for UNISA typically open in August (for following year) and close on November 30 (Semester 1); May (Semester 2). We strongly recommend applying as early as possible, as popular programmes fill up quickly. Visit https://www.unisa.ac.za/sites/myunisa/default/Apply-to-UNISA to apply online.

faqs.question: How much is the application fee for UNISA?
faqs.answer: The application fee at UNISA is R115 per module (registration fee). Some students may qualify for a fee waiver — check the UNISA website for details. The application fee is usually non-refundable even if your application is unsuccessful.

faqs.question: Does UNISA accept NSFAS?
faqs.answer: Yes, UNISA is an accredited NSFAS institution. If your household income is below R350,000 per year, you may qualify for NSFAS funding which can cover tuition, accommodation, meals, transport, and a living allowance. Apply at www.nsfas.org.za from October each year.

faqs.question: What documents do I need to apply to UNISA?
faqs.answer: For your application to UNISA, you will need: a certified copy of your ID, certified copies of your Grade 11 and/or Grade 12 results, proof of residence, and any programme-specific requirements (portfolio for arts, HPAT scores for health sciences, etc.). All documents should be certified within 3 months of the application date.
